economic_notes: Nation participates in Cascade Power Project (900MW natural gas power plant near Edson, AB) through Kehewin Power Trust via Indigenous Communities Syndicate LP. AIOC provided $93M in loan guarantees. Nation has interests in oilfield services companies (Seven Lakes Oilfield Services LP, Pimee Well Servicing LP, Pimee Well Services Ltd.), contracting (Kwayaskwaw Contracting Ltd.), gasification (681415 Alberta Ltd.), and general store (Pimee Kehewin General Store LP). Gas royalties and oil and gas revenues administered by Government of Canada under Indian Oil and Gas Act.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Three trusts (Kehewin Legacy Trust, Kehewin Treaty Land Entitlement Trust, Kehewin Specific Claims Settlement Trust) are included as unincorporated entities rather than fully consolidated subsidiaries. Three business entities (681415 Alberta Ltd., Kwayaskwaw Contracting Ltd., Kehewin Power Trust) use modified equity method. Three additional entities use cost method. Net financial assets figure on balance sheet shows -2,014,698 but Statement of Change in Net Financial Assets shows -2,014,697 (rounding difference). Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($34,426,803) + Tribal Chiefs ($1,414,379) + First Nation and Inuit Health Benefits ($3,897,486) + CMHC ($409,179) + Government of Alberta ($233,144) + FNDF ($751,110) = $41,132,101 before deferred revenue adjustments; net revenue reported is $36,433,425. OSR calculated as Other revenue + Rental income + Income from funds held in trust = $4,340,623. Term loans due on demand ($6,217,804) classified separately from long-term debt. Unrealized loss on Legacy Trust mutual funds recorded in year.
